sections.title: 10.2Resource Media - Content
sections.introduction:
A photo, video, or audio recording acquired or used in healthcare. The actual content may be inline or provided by direct reference.
This resource is referenced by AdverseEvent , ClinicalImpression , DeviceUseStatement , DiagnosticReport , ImagingStudy , MedicationKnowledge and Observation
sections.Scope and Usage:
The Media resource contains photos, videos, and audio recordings. It is used with media acquired or used as part of the healthcare process. Here are some typical usages:
Photos and videos of diagnostic or care provision procedures for recording purposes Images contained in diagnostic reports
sections.Boundaries and Relationships:
This resource captures a specific type of Observation - an Observation whose value is audio, video or image data.  
This resource is the preferred representation of such forms of information as it exposes the metadata relevant for 
interpreting the information.  However, in some legacy environments, media information may occasionally appear in 
Observation instead.  Systems should be aware of this possibility.
The Media resource is able to contain medical images in a DICOM format. These images may also be made accessible 
through an ImagingStudy resource, which provides a direct reference to the
image to a WADO-RS server .
For such images, the WADO-RS framework is a preferred method for representing the images - the WADO-RS
service may include rendering the image with annotations and display parameters from an associated DICOM 
presentation state, for instance.
On the other hand, the media resource allows for a robust transfer of an image across boundaries where
the WADO-RS service is not available. For this reason, medical images can also be represented in 
a Media resource, but the Media.content.url should provide a reference to a source WADO-RS service 
for the image.

definitions:
Media: a photo, video, or audio recording acquired or used in healthcare. the actual content may be inline or provided by direct reference. [STOP]
Media.identifier: identifiers associated with the image - these may include identifiers for the image itself, identifiers for the context of its collection (e.g. series ids) and context ids such as accession numbers or other workflow identifiers. [STOP]
Media.basedOn: a procedure that is fulfilled in whole or in part by the creation of this media. [STOP]
Media.partOf: a larger event of which this particular event is a component or step. [STOP]
Media.status: the current state of the {{title}}. [STOP]
Media.type: a code that classifies whether the media is an image, video or audio recording or some other media category. [STOP]
Media.modality: details of the type of the media - usually, how it was acquired (what type of device). if images sourced from a dicom system, are wrapped in a media resource, then this is the modality. [STOP]
Media.view: the name of the imaging view e.g. lateral or antero-posterior (ap). [STOP]
Media.subject: who/what this media is a record of. [STOP]
Media.encounter: the encounter that establishes the context for this media. [STOP]
Media.created[x]: the date and time(s) at which the media was collected. [STOP]
Media.issued: the date and time this version of the media was made available to providers, typically after having been reviewed. [STOP]
Media.operator: the person who administered the collection of the image. [STOP]
Media.reasonCode: describes why the event occurred in coded or textual form. [STOP]
Media.bodySite: indicates the site on the subject's body where the observation was made (i.e. the target site). [STOP]
Media.deviceName: the name of the device / manufacturer of the device  that was used to make the recording. [STOP]
Media.device: the device used to collect the media. [STOP]
Media.height: height of the image in pixels (photo/video). [STOP]
Media.width: width of the image in pixels (photo/video). [STOP]
Media.frames: the number of frames in a photo. this is used with a multi-page fax, or an imaging acquisition context that takes multiple slices in a single image, or an animated gif. if there is more than one frame, this shall have a value in order to alert interface software that a multi-frame capable rendering widget is required. [STOP]
Media.duration: the duration of the recording in seconds - for audio and video. [STOP]
Media.content: the actual content of the media - inline or by direct reference to the media source file. [STOP]
Media.note: comments made about the media by the performer, subject or other participants. [STOP]

documentation.Media: a photo, video, or audio recording acquired or used in healthcare. the actual content may be inline or provided by direct reference.
documentation.Media.identifier: identifiers associated with the image - these may include identifiers for the image itself, identifiers for the context of its collection (e.g. series ids) and context ids such as accession numbers or other workflow identifiers.
documentation.Media.basedOn: a procedure that is fulfilled in whole or in part by the creation of this media.
documentation.Media.partOf: a larger event of which this particular event is a component or step.
documentation.Media.type: a code that classifies whether the media is an image, video or audio recording or some other media category.
documentation.Media.modality: details of the type of the media - usually, how it was acquired (what type of device). if images sourced from a dicom system, are wrapped in a media resource, then this is the modality.
documentation.Media.view: the name of the imaging view e.g. lateral or antero-posterior (ap).
documentation.Media.subject: who/what this media is a record of.
documentation.Media.encounter: the encounter that establishes the context for this media.
documentation.Media.created[x]: the date and time(s) at which the media was collected.
documentation.Media.issued: the date and time this version of the media was made available to providers, typically after having been reviewed.
documentation.Media.operator: the person who administered the collection of the image.
documentation.Media.reasonCode: describes why the event occurred in coded or textual form.
documentation.Media.bodySite: indicates the site on the subject's body where the observation was made (i.e. the target site).
documentation.Media.deviceName: the name of the device / manufacturer of the device  that was used to make the recording.
documentation.Media.device: the device used to collect the media.
documentation.Media.height: height of the image in pixels (photo/video).
documentation.Media.width: width of the image in pixels (photo/video).
documentation.Media.frames: the number of frames in a photo. this is used with a multi-page fax, or an imaging acquisition context that takes multiple slices in a single image, or an animated gif. if there is more than one frame, this shall have a value in order to alert interface software that a multi-frame capable rendering widget is required.
documentation.Media.duration: the duration of the recording in seconds - for audio and video.
documentation.Media.content: the actual content of the media - inline or by direct reference to the media source file.
documentation.Media.note: comments made about the media by the performer, subject or other participants.
